title: AN ACT DIVIDING BARANGAY PAMPANGA IN THE CITY OF DAVAO INTO THREE (3) DISTINCT AND INDEPENDENT BARANGAYS TO BE KNOWN AS BAEANGAY PAMPANGA, BARANGAY ALFONSO ANGLIONGTO, SR., AND BARANGAY VICENTE HIZON, SR.

chunks.text:
113 OG No. 46, 8195 (November 13, 2017); Manila Bulletin, November 3, 2017

[ REPUBLIC ACT NO. 10953, October 30, 2017 ]

AN ACT DIVIDING BARANGAY PAMPANGA IN THE CITY OF DAVAO INTO THREE (3) DISTINCT AND INDEPENDENT BARANGAYS TO BE KNOWN AS BAEANGAY PAMPANGA, BARANGAY ALFONSO ANGLIONGTO, SR., AND BARANGAY VICENTE HIZON, SR.

Be it enacted by the Senate and House of Representatives of the

Philippines in Congress assembled:

SECTION 1.

Division of Barangay Parnpcmga. -

Barangay Pampsnga in the City of Davao is hereby divided into three (3) distinct and independent barangays to bs known as: (1) Barangay Pampanga; (2) Barangay Alfonso Angliongto, Sr.; and (3) Barangay Vicente Hizon, Sr.

SEC. 2.

Territorial Boundaries. -

The territorial boundaries of each barangay created in the immediately preceding section shall be as follows:

(1) Barangay Pampanga

Bounded on the Northeast (NE), pts. 1 to 9, by Barangay Sasa, Case 16;

On the Southeast (SE), pts. 9 to 11, by Barangay Sasa, Case 16;

On the Southwest (SW), pts. 12 to 21, by the shoreline of Davao Gulf; and

On the Northwest (NW), pts. 22 to pt. 1, by Barangay Vicente Hizon, Sr., Case 14.

The technical description of Barangay Pampanga is as follows:

(3) Barangay Vicente Hizon, Sr.

Bounded on the Northeast (NE) and Southeast (SE), pts. 1-11, by Barangay Pampanga, Case 15;

On the Southwest (SW), pts. 12-13, by shoreline 44A Davao Gulf;

On the South (S), pts. 13-17, all of Barangay Lapu-Lapu, Case 10;

On the Southwest (SW), point 18, by Barangay Castillo, Case 11;

On the West (W), pts. 19-21, by Barangay Angliongto, Case 13; and

On the Northwest (NW), pts. 22-29, by Barangay Sasa, Case 16.

The technical description of Barangay Vicente Hizon, Sr. is as follows:

SEC. 3.

Conduct and Supervision of Plebiscite.

- The plebiscite conducted and supervised by the Commission on Elections (COMELEC) in Barangay Pampanga on July 25, 2004, pursuant to City Ordinance No. 00161-03 of the Sangguniang Panlungsod of the City of Davao, which ratified the creation of Barangay Alfonso Angliongto, Sr. and of Barangay Vicente Hizon, Sr., shall serve as substantial compliance with the plebiscite requirement under Section 10 of Republic Act No. 7160, as amended, otherwise known as the "Local Government Code of 1991".

SEC. 4.

Incumbent Barangay Officials.

- The incumbent barangay officials of barangays Pampanga, Alfonso Angliongto, Sr., and Vicente Hizon, Sr., City of Davao shall continue to hold office until such time that their successors have been duly elected and qualified.

SEC. 5.

Public Infrastructure and Facilities.

- All public infrastructure and facilities for public use existing therein at the time of the approval of this Act are hereby transferred without cost or compensation to and shall be administered by the new barangays.

SEC. 6.

Internal Revenue Allotment Shares.

- Barangays Pampanga, Alfonso Angliongto, Sr. and Vicente Hizon, Sr. shall be entitled to Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) shares pursuant to Section 285 of Republic Act No. 7160, as amended, otherwise known as the "Local Government Code of 1991".

SEC. 7.

Separability Clause.

- If, for any reason, any part or provision of this Act is declared unconstitutional or invalid, other parts or provisions which are not affected thereby shall continue to be in full force and effect.

SEC. 8.

Effectivity.

- This Act shall take effect fifteen (15) days after its publication in the Official Gazette or in a newspaper of general circulation.

Approved,

(SGD.)

AQUILINO "KOKO" PIMENTEL III

President of the Senate

(SGD.)

PANTALEON D. ALVAREZ

Speaker of the House of Representatives

This Act which originated in the House of Representatives was passed by the House of Representatives on March 13, 2017, amended by the Senate on August 14, 2017, and which amendments were concurred in by the House of Representatives on September 6, 2017.

(SGD.)

LUTGARDO B. BARBO

Secretary of the Senate

(SGD.)

CESAR STRAIT PAREJA

Secretary General

House of Representatives

Approved: OCT 30 2017

(SGD.)

RODRIGO ROA DUTERTE

President of the Philippines
